Summary: All External emails blocked to GlobalNOC mail ID and GNOC members (NSI)
Description: All external emails blocked to GlobaNoc and it's members Incident Summary Issue: Email delivery failures to globalnoc@semtech.com mailbox and its members due to Mimecast email filtering system. Root Cause: Emails were being held/blocked by Mimecast's new email filtering system. External and internal senders are currently being screened as part of postimplementation review. No senders are whitelisted at this time while the email security team reviews and organizes the new filtering system. Current Status: GNOC Leadership will be joining a joint call today, 20251009, with the Semtech Global IT team admins to persue a permanent resolution. Our current (individual) workaround, is a manual release of held emails after logging into Mimecast, https://login.mimecast.com/m/portal/login/#/login The team received only one general notification email from Mimecast last month , informing them about the new email filtering system implementation and asking them to review the Mimecast portal. However, this was merely a broad announcement about the system rollout rather than specific alerts about blocked emails. Impact: Multiple emails were held in quarantine and not delivered to recipients Priority customer service emails were delayed, affecting realtime response capabilities No notification emails were received by affected users when messages were held Resolution Steps Taken: All held emails were manually released by the Email Admin team Emails were released specifically for globalnoc@semtech.com shared mailbox and its members Users were informed about the Mimecast portal ( https://loginusb.mimecast.com/ ) where they can review and release held/blocked/rejected emails independently Current Status: Emails have been released (temporary fix) Permanent whitelisting is not possible at this time due to ongoing review of the Mimecast system Postimplementation work is being handled by the Email Admin team 1 0/10–Asim and Arabinda have provide a plenary listing of external domains that Semt

Summary: Alert: COFI-3629 Customer Presence Verte Data session issue following 4G-3G handover Over Orange 901 (CSI)

GNOC management has instructed the manager to contact them within the next 15–30 minutes. Summary: HC: Lost Services Increasing for Orange_901(CSI)
Description: On October 07, 2025, at 07:35 UTC, during a routine health check, GNOC observed a significant increase in lost service. We have investigated the issue and found the following observations: Investigation & Findings: OpenSearch analysis indicated sessions were stopped due to Lost Service towards Orange901 , impacting multiple VPLMNs and customers. Summary: ALERT:  Jersey Telecom LTE Network Degradation (primarily in North America) (CSI)
Description: Jersey Telecom LTE Network Degradation (primarily in North America) | No Customer Reported Cases Decrease Jersey Telecom LTE Network Connectivity JT’s LTE traffic dropped 50% Impact: Essentials 3 customers may have experienced some issues (delays or intermittent failures) establishing LTE Data services and network connectivity primarily in the North America market. Approximately a total of 9.8K LTE Data Sessions were dropped. Most existing Data Sessions were unaffected. Cause: One of JT’s customers experienced failures on their primary IMSI provider causing congestion overloading JT’s IPX links. Velos (JT) ticket# IOT42932 Final Update: Services Restored. Jersey Telecom (JT) had experienced network degradation in LTE Network Service primarily in North America. JT experienced a Device Connectivity Issue due to congestion on one of its partner’s core network. The Incident was raised to a Level 3 Escalation. An official Velos (JT) RCA will be requested. GNOC engaged the partner via the Velos IoT Support team pushing the carrier to address and resolve. At 20251009 15:38 UTC there was a significant drop in Jersey Telecom LTE Network Traffic and Connectivity. At least half of the normal traffic levels are being observed. GNOC engaged the carrier Velos IoT Support (Jersey Telecom) to investigate the possible issue or unexpected network changes on its network. Start Time: 20251009 15:38 UTC End Time: 20251009 22:05 UTC Duration: 6 hours, 27 minutes Impacted Customer(s): Connected Cycle, Premier Wireless Solutions, NextCentury Submetering Systems, LLCCorporate Systems Engineering Connectivity, Premier Wireless Solutions, UHS Pty Ltd (KIDDE Global Solutions) US, Wilson Electronics ICCID(s) sampled: 8933240100000951505, 89332401000012715088 Internal IMSI: 234507089847846, 234500020162124 MSISDN: 14696340119, 337000020205516 Impacted VPLMN: TMobile USA, Inc, AT&T Mobility, Verizon Wireless, Rogers Wireless, and Bouygues Telecom Impacted Roaming Partner: Jersey Telecom Country:

Summary: ALERT:  High Memory usage on FWL-FKT-FRT-Node0 Firewall (NSI)
Description: Summary: On 29th September at 15:46 UTC, GNOC received a P3 ALERT related to “High Memory usage on FWLFKTFRTNode0 Firewall”. GNOC opened this ticket & reached out to SME for a fix. SME Hafedh investigated & found that, the misconfiguration on DNS Side is generating hundreds of denied DNS queries per hour , resulting in, Log buffer saturation , Sustained memory elevation , & ACL processing overhead . Hence the System Team, from Juniper needs to rectify the DNS settings to fix the issue. Currently alert has autoclosed, however the memory usage is still high. Current Memory Usage: 79% (baseline: 5657%) . Current Status→ System team needs to correct DNS configuration on 10.62.55.22 ( SME Hafedh will give feedback when he has a confirmation from system team , as per him there is no urgencies, he is monitoring the FWL) There is no ETR received from SME I just emailed SME (Hafedh Essay), requesting any possible ETR he may have from System Team on rectifying DNS configuration. Analysis from SME: Required Action: System team must reconfigure DNS settings on server 10.62.55.22 to use authorized DNS servers: Primary: 10.62.30.100 Secondary: 10.62.30.101 Expected outcome: Memory should return to baseline (5657%) within 24 hours after DNS correction. Next Steps: System team corrects DNS configuration on 10.62.55.22 NOC monitors firewall memory levels If memory remains elevated after 4 hours, secondary investigation required (SNMP polling load or other factors) Current Status 10/10 Just pinged SME via this JSM ticket since the Memory Usage levels have decreased down to 66% as of 1:00 CST/6:00 UTC today, the 10th. Requested confirmation that DNS config was rectified. GNOC Observation: GNOC checked in ZENOSS & observed below percent of memory usage again different days. Before 17th Sept 06:00 UTC – 56% On 17th Sept 12:00 UTC ~ 62% 18th September to 29th September ~ 59% From 29th September 18:00 UTC till date (8th October 07:00 UTC) ~ 79% (Alert is Closed)

Summary: NAM - Syniverse GRX redundancy project
Description: CAB is to enable the additional 10 gig fibers we have with Syniverse. This is a fortification of our Syniverse connectivity and gives us much greater redundancy. No MOP has been added as all the work is on the Syniverse side to open traffic; we just need to monitor traffic (PGW sessions, and SS7 links to TMM). The CAB is for NAM region only and will not impact traffic to EU PGW / EU SS7 / EU Diameter Links.

David pointed out that there is some sort of alerting remapping that moves these alert types from P5 to P1 for some reason: Note: This Nutanix service is designed to allow Jumphost redundant availability. If one Jumphost is unavailable, Nutanix allows for the switching of the next available Jumphost.

Summary: Alert Improvement : Location Update thresholds for Each Roaming Partner
Description: Greetings !!! Many times GNOC receive different P2 alerts , which subsequently ends up with Service degradation for our customers in most of the cases. The opsGenie alert we get shows overall combined KPIs for different Roaming partner. It does not specify which Roaming partner has issues or thresholds not met. So from GNOC perspective we tend to check all Roaming partner KPIs when we receive such alert. If we can have Individual Roaming partner’s Location update threshold set & if we get opsGenie alert specifying which Roaming Partner has issues/threshold not met then it will save us a lot of time. Kindly review the request. ZENOSSLink Example of alerts → PS Update location Ratio too low CS Update location Ratio too low Cancel location update ratio too low etc.

Summary: Post-Network Change Exposed ongoing apn issue for users through Sparkle on Orange France
Description: Starting at 16:29 UTC on , GNOC began receiving alerts for a signaling issue on Orange 901. After further investigation, the increase in signaling was from RNA. I have discussed this issue with Minh, and he stated that they made a change for the customer Presence Verte on CAB COFI3629. This caused the RNA on Orange 901, and since these are advanced 3 and advanced 4 Sims, the traffic moved over to Sparkle. According to the SME, when traffic was moved over to Sparkle, the devices were unable to create sessions over LTE, and we observed an increase in purges. We have sent an email to Sparkle to investigate. Current Status: GNOC has reopened this ticket. Anas conducted testing and is still observing issues with Sparkle. He has reengaged Sparkle via email and is awaiting their investigation. GNOC just sent over to Sparkle a relevant pcap, showing the purge come in immediately after the successful ULR; GNOC also included a list of IMSIs and a jpeg file showing the surge in Diameter UE purges. As of 8:10 UTC, COFI ticket 3629 states the block was reversed. Sparkle still has their ticket open (CSFA 00652929 ), and GNOC leadership discussed with Sparkle managers the morning of 10/8. They are escalating with Orange France, and a batch of IMSIs have been shared with Sparkle along with a couple of pcaps revealing the S6 purges. Querying the first 10% of the IMSI list within AV IDed that 24 out of 28 (202 total imsi list) are Presence Verte customers. Purges via Sparkle have decreased drastically but due to the block reversal, we are not likely to capture the S6 purges occur to the degree they transpired yesterday, via Sparkle on Orange 20801 Current Status 10/10 Semtech still has credible reason to believe CSREQs are NOT coming through via Sparkle, originating on Orange France 020801); 10/10 testing (Anas) with 208243092590247 / 222013094590247 revealed successful GTP traffic with internet.swir but not internet.mbqt.net More recent was cut today to tie into open COFI, 3629. Summary: ALERT: Sparkle Network Connectivity Incident (CSI)
Description: Sparkle Network Connectivity Incident | No Customer Tickets Reported Between 20250702 22:07 23:43 UTC, Essentials 1 subscription offers in the EU and NAM markets may have experienced delays and failures in SMS, USSD, Voice, and Data service establishment, as well as network connectivity. Although Sparkle’s incident lasted 1 hour, 36 minutes, the first 45 minutes of existing Data Sessions were impacted until 23:07 UTC. At 23:07 UTC, Data Service recovered; Existing Data sessions were unaffected, however, there may have been delays establishing new sessions. For the entirety of the incident, Essentials 1 customers may have experienced delays in SMS, USSD, Voice, Data service establishment, and network connectivity. After 23:06 UTC, existing Data sessions were unaffected; however, there may be delays establishing new sessions. Note (from PostIncident Assessment): In accordance with the Incident Management Process, GNOC management has reclassified the incident (lowered severity) from a Priority 2 (P2) to a Priority 3 (P3). For context, ChargePoint—the largest customer utilizing Essentials 1 on the Sparkle network—experienced minimal impact, with a loss of no more than 1,320 data sessions during the brief incident. Final Update: Services Restored. RCA received Sparkle's thirdparty provider restored services by repairing the Fiber circuit link between the cities of Roma and Bologna. Incident was elevated to a Level 2 carrier escalation. Semtech Data Service Timeline ( See Chargepoint Data Sessions sample ): Start Time: 20250702 22:21 UTC End Time: 20250702 23:06 UTC Duration: 45 minutes Sparkle Incident Timline Start Time: 20250702 22:07 UTC End Time: 20250702 23:43 UTC Duration: 1 hour, 36 minutes Impacted Customer(s): ChargePoint Inc, Matooma, Networth Telecom, Connected Cycle, Synox, AddSecure Group Services AB, SAVVY Telematic Systems AG, Nextivity, Allcom Telecom Brazil – Reseller, Ingenico France, Inpro/Seal Company, Sigicom AB – Connectivity, Webb Fontaine Group FZ

Summary: SIM v4.4.1 Behaviour Issue During Telia Sweden Outage
Description: ssue Summary: During the Telia Sweden network outage on September 22, 2025 , devices using SIM v4.4.1 and connected via Sparkle failed to switch IMSI profiles or MVNOs. Approximately 2,400 Sparkleconnected devices were affected and remained without service until Telia was restored. In contrast, BICSconnected devices with the same SIM version successfully switched to alternative networks. Devices with SIM v5.x handled the situation correctly, switching IMSI and MVNO as needed. Customer Impact: The most affected customer was Eskilstuna Strängnäs Energi & Miljö (ESEM) . Out of 9,366 Advanced2 SIM v4.4.1 devices , around 25% experienced prolonged outages . Technical Investigation: ICCID 8933240100001435914 was identified as ESSENTIALS1 , despite AV showing it as ADVANCED2 . The label “SmartSIM to Easy 210101” suggests it was updated via OTA . ESSENTIALS1 uses Sparkle IMSI only , which may explain the lack of IMSI switching during the outage. Followup: Asim provided additional ICCIDs for verification: 8933240100001890344 8933240100001435159 8933240100001820472 Fred confirmed that all are ESSENTIALS1 , likely migrated from ADVANCED2 via OTA.
